target.title: feat(autoreview): add reference support
target.body:
Closes #24

## Summary
- add autoreview reference inputs for local paths, Git repositories, references files, hidden references, and strict reference mode
- wire reference access through Codex, Claude, OpenCode, and prompt-manifest fallback modes
- add reference fixture coverage to the review harness and document the workflow in the autoreview skill
- harden reference handling for local-path safety, hidden metadata redaction, dry-run behavior, pinned Git refs, file reference materialization, and alias validation

## Verification
- `python3 skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --help`
- `python3 skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --self-test-config-defaults`
- `python3 skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --self-test-engine-isolation`
- `python3 skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --self-test-opencode-isolation`
- `python3 skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --self-test-json-array-parser`
- `python3 skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --self-test-references`
- `skills/autoreview/scripts/test-review-harness --reference-fixtures`
- `python3 -m compileall -q skills/autoreview/scripts`
- `scripts/validate-skills`
- `git diff --check -- skills/autoreview/SKILL.md skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview skills/autoreview/scripts/test-review-harness.py skills/autoreview/scripts/test-review-harness skills/autoreview/scripts/test-review-harness.ps1`
- `python3 skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main --engine codex --model gpt-5.5 --thinking high` reported clean with no accepted/actionable findings

## Notes
- Optional `ruby scripts/install-skills.test.rb` still fails on the pre-existing Ruby 2.6 `assert_path_exists` incompatibility in `scripts/install-skills.test.rb`.

commands.author: SebTardif

target.title: fix: relay Claude CLI assistant partial messages as streaming deltas
target.body:
Fixes #86050

## Summary

Relay Claude CLI assistant records as streaming deltas so the WebChat UI shows progressive output instead of waiting for the full response.

**Fix 1**: Parse `assistant` records from the Claude CLI JSONL stream and forward their text content as `chat.delta` events.

**Fix 2**: Gate on `stop_reason` using a positive partial signal: only forward records where `stop_reason` is explicitly present and `null` (the documented Claude CLI partial marker). Complete messages with `stop_reason: "end_turn"` are skipped to avoid duplicating the final message. Records without `stop_reason` at all are ignored as ambiguous (could be complete messages from custom `claude-stream-json` backends).

## Changed files

- `src/agents/cli-output.ts` -- Added `parseClaudeCliPartialAssistantDelta` with positive `stop_reason: null` guard
- `src/agents/cli-output.test.ts` -- Tests covering partial forwarding, complete-message skip, absent-stop_reason rejection, and edge cases

## Verification

All tests use the documented `stop_reason: null` marker for partial messages:
- `stop_reason: null` (explicit) -> text forwarded as streaming delta
- `stop_reason: "end_turn"` -> record skipped (complete message, not a partial)
- `stop_reason` absent -> record skipped (not a confirmed partial; prevents false delivery from custom backends)
- Non-assistant record types -> ignored by the parser
- Malformed content arrays -> returns null without throwing
- Mixed text + tool_use content -> text extracted, tool_use entries ignored

Fixes #86050

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Claude CLI assistant records are not relayed as streaming deltas, so WebChat waits for the full response. The `parseClaudeCliPartialAssistantDelta` function requires a positive partial signal (`stop_reason: null` explicitly present) to skip complete messages and forward only true streaming partials as `chat.delta` events.

Real environment tested: macOS 15.5, Node v22.19, OpenClaw built from branch `fix/gateway-stream-relay` at `/tmp/proof-86649`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

Step 1. Build from branch and verify the partial assistant delta parser is active in compiled production code.
Step 2. Feed four Claude CLI JSONL records through the production `createCliJsonlStreamingParser`: two partials (`stop_reason: null`), one complete (`stop_reason: "end_turn"`), and one ambiguous (no `stop_reason` field).

Evidence after fix: terminal output from the patched build:

The partial assistant delta parser and its `stop_reason: null` guard are active in the compiled production bundle:

```console
$ grep -n "parseClaudeCliPartialAssistantDelta\|stop_reason.*null\|partialDelta" dist/claude-live-session-BYm1bWnI.js
216:function parseClaudeCliPartialAssistantDelta(params) {
220:if (!isRecord$1(message) || !("stop_reason" in message) || message.stop_reason !== null) return null;
394:const partialDelta = parseClaudeCliPartialAssistantDelta({
402:if (partialDelta) {
403:assistantText = partialDelta.text;
404:params.onAssistantDelta(partialDelta);
```

Runtime exercise of the production parser with Claude CLI JSONL event shapes:

```console
$ npx tsx proof-exercise.ts
=== Feeding Claude CLI JSONL records through production parser ===

Input record 1 (partial, stop_reason: null):
  {"type":"assistant","message":{"role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Here is"}],"stop_reason":null},"session_id":"sess-abc123"}
  -> Deltas so far: [{"text":"Here is","delta":"Here is"}]

Input record 2 (partial, stop_reason: null, extended text):
  {"type":"assistant","message":{"role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Here is the answer"}],"stop_reason":null},"session_id":"sess-abc123"}
  -> Deltas so far: [{"text":"Here is","delta":"Here is"},{"text":"Here is the answer","delta":" the answer"}]

Input record 3 (complete, stop_reason: "end_turn"):
  {"type":"assistant","message":{"role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Here is the answer to your question."}],"stop_reason":"end_turn"},"session_id":"sess-abc123"}
  -> Deltas so far: [{"text":"Here is","delta":"Here is"},{"text":"Here is the answer","delta":" the answer"}]
  -> Complete message skipped (no new delta), avoiding duplicate delivery

Input record 4 (no stop_reason field, ambiguous source):
  {"type":"assistant","message":{"role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Some response"}]},"session_id":"sess-abc123"}
  -> Deltas so far: [{"text":"Here is","delta":"Here is"},{"text":"Here is the answer","delta":" the answer"}]
  -> Record without stop_reason skipped (not a confirmed partial)

=== Summary ===
Total records fed: 4
Partial deltas relayed: 2
Delta breakdown:
  [0] delta="Here is" (cumulative: "Here is")
  [1] delta=" the answer" (cumulative: "Here is the answer")

Before fix: Records 1-2 would not emit deltas (assistant type was ignored).
After fix: Records 1-2 relay as streaming deltas. Records 3-4 correctly skipped.
No duplicate final text delivered.
```

Observed result after fix: The compiled production code at `dist/claude-live-session-BYm1bWnI.js:216-220` contains the `parseClaudeCliPartialAssistantDelta` function with the positive partial signal guard (`stop_reason !== null`). At line 394, the main parser calls this function as a fallback when no raw stream delta is available. Runtime exercise confirms: assistant records with `stop_reason: null` relay as incremental streaming deltas (`"Here is"` then `" the answer"`), complete messages with `stop_reason: "end_turn"` are skipped (preventing duplicate delivery), and ambiguous records without `stop_reason` are also skipped (preventing false delivery from custom backends).

What was not tested: Live Claude CLI session with real streaming output over the gateway WebSocket connection. The fix exercises the production parser against the documented Claude CLI JSONL protocol shapes. End-to-end WebChat/TUI display was not verified.
